physician_information.physicians_address: Riverbend Family Medicine, 2145 Riverside Avenue, Suite 300, Jacksonville, FL 32204

allergies.normal_reaction_and_management_row_1: Develops an itchy, blotchy rash on torso and arms within 12–24 hours of taking penicillin-class antibiotics. No history of anaphylaxis, breathing difficulty, or swelling of lips/tongue, but reaction has been consistent since childhood. Management: avoid all penicillin and related antibiotics (e.g., amoxicillin). If accidentally taken, discontinue medication immediately and contact prescribing physician. For mild rash, takes over-the-counter antihistamine such as cetirizine (Zyrtec) and monitors symptoms closely. Medical providers have this allergy noted in his chart, and he always reminds urgent care/ER staff before they prescribe antibiotics.
allergies.normal_reaction_and_management_row_2: Experiences typical seasonal allergic rhinitis symptoms from late March through May and again in late September, including sneezing, itchy/watery eyes, mild sinus pressure, and runny nose. No history of asthma or breathing difficulty. Manages symptoms with daily over-the-counter antihistamine (loratadine 10 mg) during peak seasons, and uses saline nasal spray in the evenings. Wears sunglasses outdoors on high-pollen days and keeps car windows closed with A/C on recirculate. Symptoms are annoying but do not limit physical activity; he is still able to hike and participate in outdoor activities with these precautions.
